channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Family Day Care Capability Trial supports regional early learning access
item.description: The Australian Government’s Family Day Care Capability Trial is testing new ways to strengthen family day care, support educator recruitment and improve access to flexible early childhood education and care in regional communities with limited supply.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government’s Family Day Care Capability Trial is testing new ways to strengthen family day care, support educator recruitment and improve access to flexible early childhood education and care in regional communities with limited supply.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Paid parental leave changes: what families and ECEC employers need to know
item.description: Australia’s Paid Parental Leave scheme is expanding, with more paid days, superannuation on government-funded payments and greater flexibility for families. For early childhood education and care employers, the changes may also affect workforce planning, rostering, staff retention, enrolment timing and centre profitability.
item.content:encoded: Australia’s Paid Parental Leave scheme is expanding, with more paid days, superannuation on government-funded payments and greater flexibility for families. For early childhood education and care employers, the changes may also affect workforce planning, rostering, staff retention, enrolment timing and centre profitability.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Big move to keep Aussie kids safe - News.com.au
item.description: Big move to keep Aussie kids safe - News.com.au
item.content:encoded: Big move to keep Aussie kids safe - News.com.au
item.dc:creator: News.com.au

item.title: QFCC scenario guide supports ECEC services to strengthen child safe practice
item.description: The Queensland Family and Child Commission has released a scenario discussion guide to help organisations, including early childhood education and care services, embed Queensland’s Child Safe Standards into everyday practice.
item.content:encoded: The Queensland Family and Child Commission has released a scenario discussion guide to help organisations, including early childhood education and care services, embed Queensland’s Child Safe Standards into everyday practice.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From bright idea to brilliant impact: reflecting on rise & shine’s kindergarten’s mobile planetarium
item.description: Three years ago, Rise & Shine Kindergarten introduced a bold and imaginative addition to its early learning program, Australia’s only childcare-operated mobile planetarium. What began as an ambitious extension of its Orbit, Centre of Imagination STEM program has since become one of the organisation’s most celebrated and impactful educational experiences.
item.content:encoded: Three years ago, Rise & Shine Kindergarten introduced a bold and imaginative addition to its early learning program, Australia’s only childcare-operated mobile planetarium. What began as an ambitious extension of its Orbit, Centre of Imagination STEM program has since become one of the organisation’s most celebrated and impactful educational experiences.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: ECU speech pathology students bring early intervention boost to the Y WA’s Yappara House ELC
item.description: Children at the Y WA’s Yappara House Early Learning Centre (ELC) are benefiting from an innovative partnership with Edith Cowan University School of Medical and Health Sciences (ECU), as speech pathology students have begun placements aimed at strengthening early language development during the most critical years of learning.
item.content:encoded: Children at the Y WA’s Yappara House Early Learning Centre (ELC) are benefiting from an innovative partnership with Edith Cowan University School of Medical and Health Sciences (ECU), as speech pathology students have begun placements aimed at strengthening early language development during the most critical years of learning.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Understanding how children learn and develop in the early years is central to supporting strong educational outcomes and lifelong wellbeing - The Sector
item.description: Understanding how children learn and develop in the early years is central to supporting strong educational outcomes and lifelong wellbeing - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Understanding how children learn and develop in the early years is central to supporting strong educational outcomes and lifelong wellbeing - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Market Failure and the need for Stewardship in Early Childhood Services: This is more about oversupply, than a loss of public confidence.
item.description: The announcement of Australia’s second largest provider of early childhood education services G8 Education will close 40 of its services due to key issues such as low occupancy, shortage of experienced educators and financial sustainability, was no surprise to anyone in the sector.
item.content:encoded: The announcement of Australia’s second largest provider of early childhood education services G8 Education will close 40 of its services due to key issues such as low occupancy, shortage of experienced educators and financial sustainability, was no surprise to anyone in the sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Investigation needed into child protection leaks
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver
item.description: SNAICC has welcomed the release of the National Child and Family Investment Strategy as a landmark framework to shift investment to ACCOs.

item.title: Supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children means supporting the community-controlled sector
item.description: Op-Ed on supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children means supporting the community-controlled sector
item.content:encoded: Op-Ed on supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children means supporting the community-controlled sector
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 17 April 2026
item.description: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.content:encoded: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: SNAICC calls for a national youth justice summit, launches Sector Transformation Principles Framework, and Chair Muriel Bamblett speaks on family violence reform.

item.title: Fixating on a ‘magic number’ of childcare hours misses what’s most important for kids’ development
item.description: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.content:encoded: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
